channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: China test-launches a ballistic missile in the South Pacific and raises regional concerns
item.description: The launch, using a dummy warhead, took place the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defense treaty meant to counter Chinese influence in the Pacific.
item.content:encoded:
The launch, using a dummy warhead, took place the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defense treaty meant to counter Chinese influence in the Pacific.

(Image credit: Li Xiangchao)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Tanker set ablaze after being struck by projectile in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iranian state television said the tanker came under attack after ignoring warnings but did not directly claim the assault.
item.content:encoded:
Iranian state television said the tanker came under attack after ignoring warnings but did not directly claim the assault.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What a divorce coach wishes couples knew before ending a marriage
item.description: Divorce is a tool, not a weapon, says Karen McNenny, author of a new book on the subject. She explains how to end a marriage while protecting your family and your mental health.
item.content:encoded:
Divorce is a tool, not a weapon, says Karen McNenny, author of a new book on the subject. She explains how to end a marriage while protecting your family and your mental health.

(Image credit: Wiley/Jossey-Bass/NPR, Nicole Wickens/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: The U.S. men's run at the World Cup ends with a 4-1 Round of 16 loss to Belgium
item.description: The team was in the eye of a storm over a controversial phone call from President Trump to FIFA's head about a red card on a U.S. striker. But even with the U.S. at full strength, Belgium easily won.
item.content:encoded:
The team was in the eye of a storm over a controversial phone call from President Trump to FIFA's head about a red card on a U.S. striker. But even with the U.S. at full strength, Belgium easily won.

(Image credit: Alex Grimm)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Extreme heat on Independence Day will be America's new normal, experts say
item.description: In cities across the U.S., parades were canceled and events were delayed because of the heat. Meanwhile, emergency rooms saw a high number of people with heat-related illnesses.
item.content:encoded:
In cities across the U.S., parades were canceled and events were delayed because of the heat. Meanwhile, emergency rooms saw a high number of people with heat-related illnesses.

(Image credit: Amid Farahi)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: This World Cup marks end of era for Ronaldo and other soccer greats
item.description: Some of the greatest footballers of a generation played their final World Cup match this summer.
item.content:encoded:
Some of the greatest footballers of a generation played their final World Cup match this summer.

(Image credit: Lars Baron)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: USA set to take on Belgium in World Cup amid Trump red card controversy
item.description: Ahead of this evening's World Cup match between the U.S. and Belgium, the drama about the eligibility of U.S. striker Folarin Balogun continues to swirl following his red card in the previous match.
item.content:encoded: Ahead of this evening's World Cup match between the U.S. and Belgium, the drama about the eligibility of U.S. striker Folarin Balogun continues to swirl following his red card in the previous match.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Supreme Court lets Texas restrict minors' access to app stores for the time being
item.description: Texas' App Store Accountability Act requires minors to have their parents' permission to download most apps. The Supreme Court says the law can go into effect as lawsuits continue in lower courts.
item.content:encoded:
Texas' App Store Accountability Act requires minors to have their parents' permission to download most apps. The Supreme Court says the law can go into effect as lawsuits continue in lower courts.

(Image credit: Paul Ellis)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Graham Platner faces growing calls to withdraw following allegation of sexual assault
item.description: The Democratic candidate for Senate in Maine said he was assessing next steps after allegations of sexual assault were reported on Monday by Politico. Platner denied them as "categorically untrue."
item.content:encoded:
The Democratic candidate for Senate in Maine said he was assessing next steps after allegations of sexual assault were reported on Monday by Politico. Platner denied them as "categorically untrue."

(Image credit: Laura Brett)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Man sues DHS after agents tracked him down for sending a scathing email to ICE
item.description: Federal agents tried to track David Streever to his home and hotel, and left him a warning notice that a critical email he sent the former head of ICE may have been illegal. Now he's suing.
item.content:encoded:
Federal agents tried to track David Streever to his home and hotel, and left him a warning notice that a critical email he sent the former head of ICE may have been illegal. Now he's suing.

(Image credit: David Streever)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block
